summary: Updated the m2ctx preprocessing flags so generated ctx.c preserves comments by passing `--passthru-comments` to pcpp. The PR was a small tooling/header-context improvement with one added line and no recorded discussion.
what_changed: The existing m2ctx pcpp configuration already passed through defines and emitted line directives; this change extends passthrough behavior to comments.
No project source files or headers were changed directly; the effect is on generated `ctx.c` output.
smart_moves: Made the comment-preservation behavior a preprocessing flag rather than modifying generated output after the fact.
Kept the tooling change minimal and localized to the existing `PCPP_FLAGS` configuration.
Preserving comments in `ctx.c` can make generated context files more informative for decompilation work without changing C source semantics.
decomp_lessons: When generated context files lose useful source annotations, check the preprocessor configuration before adding ad hoc generation logic.
For m2ctx/pcpp-based context generation, `--passthru-comments` is the relevant flag for retaining comments in the emitted context.
Small tooling changes can improve the usability of decompilation context even when they do not affect matching directly.
naming_conventions: `PCPP_FLAGS` is the central list of pcpp command-line flags in `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py`.
`ctx.c` refers to the generated context file used by decompilation tooling.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later changes to `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py` that adjust `PCPP_FLAGS` or pcpp behavior.
Search for PRs mentioning `ctx.c` to identify additional context-generation improvements.
Check whether generated `ctx.c` files after this PR retain comments as expected in downstream tooling.
